step_by_step_thinking:
Malaria relapses occur due to dormant liver stage parasites, known as hypnozoites. These are commonly associated with P. vivax and P. ovale infections but not typically seen in P. malariae or P. falciparum cases post-eradication of hepatic stages.

The correct option is C (Plasmodium vivax and ovale). Clinical Reasoning:

Malaria relapses happen when residual liver-stage parasites reactivate after an initial infection has been cleared by antimalarial drugs. This phenomenon primarily affects Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium ovale because they both have persistent latent forms within hepatocytes that can re-emerge weeks or months later causing recurrence.
